latest.title: Snakes can detect warm prey in the dark
latest.script_key: snakes can sense heat when light disappears watch the head before the strike because some snakes have heat sensitive pits that detect warm bodies that gives them a second kind of vision at night the target glows to them without glowing to us which reptile sense next

latest.title: Teide volcano hides a lava trick you missed
latest.script_key: lava doesn t just explode it also builds land from below teide s flank quietly pushes up new rock like a slow punch you can see the cracks where it s happening right now that s why the mountain keeps growing even when it s quiet ever stood on a volcano tell me where

latest.title: Dead desert trees reveal a root trick most miss
latest.script_key: this tree s still alive look close at the bark those cracks don t just dry it out they re air vents because under this sand its roots run sideways until they hit water that s why it survives months with no rain i didn t know trees did this until i saw their roots stretch like this

latest.title: Sharks are friendly social fish you didn't expect
latest.script_key: i love this one that guy s not in danger bull sharks in fiji pick their own buddies they remember who they like and who they don t watch their tail movements sync up that s the social signal no lone killer just fish with friends would you swim with a shark if you knew it chose you

latest.title: Black bears walk roads to save energy
latest.script_key: this bear isn t lost it s cutting corners see how its path follows the road s slope bears save energy walking downhill that s why they hug curves instead of climbing straight up when the road slopes they barely lift a foot would you take the long way if it meant less work

latest.title: The moon keeps one face turned toward Earth
latest.script_key: the moon shows us one face for a reason watch the moon s position because its rotation and orbit are locked together it spins once in about the same time it circles earth so the same side keeps facing us that is synchronized motion not stillness which orbit clue next
